text: Globalization of today can be understood with the help of global value chains (GVCs) and we derive pros and cons for the Central and Eastern European (CEE) economies. The goal of this article is to outline the challenges that countries in the region are facing and to signal the strategic choices for public policy in order to address them properly. We concentrate on the technological factor which is responsible for the decisions concerning high value adding activities in the GVCs. We refer to appropriate indicators and GVC integration indicators along with literature review in order to confirm our theoretical conclusions.
